example_prompt: Add Orange Money Mali web payment to my checkout flow: obtain an OAuth2 Bearer token, create a payment session, store the returned pay_token / notif_token, then redirect the user to payment_url.

merchant_key.description: Merchant key issued by Orange Money. Distinct from the OAuth credentials. Must be sent in the request body of every payment creation call.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code. For Mali use 'XOF'. The currency must match the country segment in the endpoint URL (here: /ml/).

order_id.description: Unique identifier for this transaction in your own system. MUST be unique per attempt — reusing the same order_id returns HTTP 403.

amount.description: Amount to charge, in the smallest unit of the currency. XOF has no decimals — pass an integer (e.g. 1000 for 1 000 FCFA).

return_url.description: HTTPS URL where the customer is redirected after a successful payment.

cancel_url.description: HTTPS URL where the customer is redirected if they cancel the payment.

notif_url.description: HTTPS URL Orange Money POSTs to asynchronously when the transaction reaches a final state. MUST be publicly reachable from the internet — Orange Money does not retry failed deliveries.

lang.description: Language of the hosted payment page. Common values: 'fr', 'en'. Defaults to 'fr' if omitted.

reference.description: Free-text label echoed back in the dashboard and in the notification payload. Useful for human-readable reconciliation. Distinct from order_id.

status.description: HTTP status echoed in the body. 201 on success.

pay_token.description: Server-side token identifying this payment session within Orange Money. Store it to query the transaction status later.

payment_url.description: URL of the hosted Orange Money payment page. Redirect the user's browser to this URL to complete the payment.

notif_token.description: One-time token Orange Money will include in the asynchronous notification POST to your notif_url. Use it to authenticate the webhook payload server-side.

code.description: Error code. Common cases: 401 (invalid Bearer), 403 (duplicated order_id or invalid merchant_key), 400 (missing/invalid field).

description.description: Optional longer description of the error returned by the OAuth or webpayment endpoint.

gotchas: Two distinct credentials are required: ORANGE_MONEY_BASIC_TOKEN (base64 of clientId:clientSecret, used to obtain the OAuth Bearer) and ORANGE_MONEY_MERCHANT_KEY (sent in the request body). They are NOT interchangeable.
order_id MUST be unique across all attempts. Reusing a previous order_id returns HTTP 403 Forbidden — generate a fresh value (e.g. UUID) for every retry, even after a failure.
amount is an integer in the smallest unit of the currency. XOF has no sub-unit, so '1000' means 1 000 FCFA, not 10.00 FCFA. Sending a decimal is rejected.

notif_url must be a publicly reachable HTTPS endpoint. Orange Money does not retry on failure — if your endpoint times out or returns a non-2xx, the notification is lost and you must reconcile manually.

The OAuth access token returned by /oauth/v3/token is short-lived. Request a fresh token before each create_payment call (or cache it with a safety margin under the documented expires_in) — do not embed a long-lived token in code.

The endpoint path is country-specific: /orange-money-webpay/ml/v1/webpayment for Mali. Other countries (SN, CI, BF, etc.) use a different path segment — do not assume one URL covers all Orange Money markets.
Always verify the payment server-side via the notif_url callback (using notif_token) before fulfilling the order. The browser redirect to return_url alone can be spoofed and is not proof of payment.
